---
title: "DA research"
author: "Nancy Tran"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-18 to 2026-"
institute: "Denison University"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: lux # Check here for more themes: https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-themes.html
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Code"
    code-copy: hover
    link-external-newwindow: true
    tbl-cap-location: top
    fig-cap-location: bottom

self-contained: true
editor: source
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# DO NOT EDIT THIS

knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.align = 'center')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(out.width = '90%')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(results = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.show = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(error = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 1.1, 4.1))

hooks = knitr::knit_hooks$get()
hook_foldable = function(type) {
  force(type)
  function(x, options) {
    res = hooks[[type]](x, options)
    
    if (isFALSE(options[[paste0("fold.", type)]])) return(res)
    
    paste0(
      "<details open><summary>", gsub("^p", "P", gsub("^o", "O", type)), "</summary>\n\n",
      res,
      "\n\n</details>"
    )
  }
}

knitr::knit_hooks$set(
  output = hook_foldable("output"),
  plot = hook_foldable("plot")
)
```

# Import Library

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(caret)
library(ranger)
library(xgboost)
library(Matrix)
library(ggplot2)
library(geodata)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(tidyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest) 
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(broom)
```

# Dataset
```{r}
#Import dataset
soc_data <- read.csv("Data/clean1.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

#factor long and lat --> numeric
soc_data$lon<-as.numeric(soc_data$Longtitude)
soc_data$lat<-as.numeric(soc_data$Latitude)

#create longitude and latitude points for merging dataset 
pts_sf <- st_as_sf(soc_data, coords = c("lon","lat"),crs=4326) 
```

The pre-clean version of SOC data (from google sheet) has 4341 observations, 20 variables.

# Adding temperature and precipitation data from CHELSA portal (import from raster files)
```{r}
urls <- readLines("Data/envidatS3paths.txt")
target_urls <- urls[grepl("bio1_|bio12_", urls)]
for (u in target_urls) {
  dest <- file.path("data/chelsa", basename(u))
  if (!file.exists(dest)) download.file(u, dest, mode="wb")
}

# Load Chelsa Rasters
r_bio1 <-rast("../CHELSA_bio1_1981-2010_V.2.1.tif")
r_bio12 <- rast("../CHELSA_bio12_1981-2010_V.2.1.tif")

#Extract & merge with original data
bio <- c(r_bio1, r_bio12)

#merge datasets
names(bio) <- c("bio1_temp", "bio12_precip")
vals <- terra::extract(bio, vect(pts_sf))
soc_data_climate <- cbind(soc_data, vals)
```

After the above code chunk, the annual average temperature and annual average 

From the depth variation, we classify depth into 3 categories: 
- Depth 0-30cm
- Depth 30-60cm
- Depth 60+ cm
First, using the package 'stringr', we split the depth into lower and upper bound by taking the sum of the first measure and the last measure of the depth class, divided by 2, then classify them into 3 categories. 

# Divide depth into 3 class categories

```{r}
soc_data_climate <- soc_data_climate %>%
  # split depth into lower/upper bounds
  mutate(depth_low  = as.numeric(str_extract(depth, "^[0-9]+")),
         depth_high = as.numeric(str_extract(depth, "[0-9]+$")),
         depth_mid  = (depth_high + depth_high) / 2) %>%
  # classify into bins
  mutate(depth_class = case_when(
    depth_mid <= 30 ~ "0-30",
    depth_mid > 30 & depth_mid <= 60 ~ "30-60",
    depth_mid > 60 ~ "60+",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

#Checking distribution of depth class
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$depth_class))
```
The observation mostly still falls under 0-30cm depth. 


# ANOVA check for log_gkg distribution per SOC distribution
```{r}
#by(gkg$log_SOC_gKg, gkg$depth_class, shapiro.test)
```
All 3 depth levels (0-30, 30-60, 60+) have p <0.001, meaning residuals are not normally distributed. Non-normality likely due to skew in SOC data --> log transformation helps, but large sample sizes make even mild deviations significant. 
 
### move this chunk here below (no dataset found here)
```{r}
# ANOVA (one-way)
#aov1 <- aov(log_SOC_gKg ~ depth_class, data = gkg)
#summary(aov1)
```
F(2, 2696) = 239.5 (with p<0.001) so depth has a strong effect on SOC & there are significant differences in mean SOC (log scale) across depth classes. This means that SOC concentrations differ significantly among 3 depths. shallower soils have higher SOC. 

# Checking distribution of numeric continuous variables: precipitation, temperature
```{r}
plot_data <- soc_data_climate %>%
  select(bio1_temp, bio12_precip) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(bio1_temp, bio12_precip),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(variable = recode(variable,
                           bio1_temp = "Mean Annual Temperature (°C)",
                           bio12_precip = "Mean Annual Precipitation (mm)"))

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = value, fill = variable)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30,
                 color = "white",
                 alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Mean Annual Temperature (°C)" = "#E64B35",
    "Mean Annual Precipitation (mm)" = "#4DBBD5"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Climate Variables",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

At lower elevation with warmer temperatures, chemical weathering processes such as hydro and oxidation tend to be more rapid. This results in deeper soils rich in clay minerals, and secondary oxides --> more microbial activity, organic matter decomposes faster and is incorporated into mineral soil horizons


# Convert SOC unit because they are not consistent 

```{r}
# Convert SOC units into concentration (by mass)
soc_data_climate$SOC_gKg <- with(soc_data_climate,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "%", soc.value * 10,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_kg", soc.value,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_g", soc.value * 1000,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "mg_g", soc.value,                # 1 mg/g = 1 g/kg
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "mg_kg", soc.value / 1000, NA))))))
 
# Convert SOC units into stock (by area)
soc_data_climate$SOC_MgHa <- with(soc_data_climate,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "Mg_ha", soc.value,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_m2", soc.value * 0.01,   # 1 g/m² = 0.01 Mg/ha
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "kg_m2", soc.value * 10,    # 1 kg/m² = 10 Mg/ha
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "kg_ha", soc.value / 1000,  # 1 kg/ha = 0.001 Mg/ha
  NA)))))
```

# Log Transformation the SOC unit because there are super huge outliers

```{r}
# inspect
summary(soc_data_climate$SOC_MgHa)
summary(soc_data_climate$SOC_gKg)

# log transform function
safe_log <- function(x, add = NULL) {
  # add: if NULL, compute minimal shift so all values > 0
  if (is.null(add)) {
    minv <- min(x[is.finite(x)], na.rm = TRUE)
    # if minv <= 0, choose shift = abs(minv) + tiny
    add <- ifelse(minv <= 0, abs(minv) + 1e-6, 0)
  }
  log(x + add)
}

# create transformed variable
soc_unit <- soc_data_climate %>%
  mutate(
    SOC_MgHa_finite = ifelse(is.finite(SOC_MgHa), SOC_MgHa, NA_real_),
    SOC_gKg_finite = ifelse(is.finite(SOC_gKg), SOC_gKg, NA_real_)
  )
# Transform both SOC_MgHa and SOC_gKg
min_SOC_mgha <- min(soc_data$SOC_MgHa_finite, na.rm = TRUE)
min_SOC_gkg <- min(soc_data$SOC_gkg_finite, na.rm = TRUE)
shift_mgha <- ifelse(min_SOC_mgha <= 0, abs(min_SOC_mgha) + 1e-6, 0)
shift_gkg <- ifelse(min_SOC_gkg <= 0, abs(min_SOC_gkg) + 1e-6, 0)

soc_unit <- soc_unit %>%
  mutate(
    log_SOC_MgHa = safe_log(SOC_MgHa_finite, add = shift_mgha),
    log_SOC_gKg = safe_log(SOC_gKg_finite, add = shift_gkg)
  )
```

# Summary log measurement of SOC_MgHa (stock)
```{r}
summary(soc_unit$log_SOC_MgHa)
```

# Summary log measurement of SOC_gKg (concentration)
```{r}
summary(soc_unit$log_SOC_gKg)
```

# SOC overtime by country (not good graph)

## First, create the soc_main dataset
```{r}
soc_main <- soc_unit %>%
  mutate(
    depth_class = factor(depth_class, ordered = FALSE),
    main_descriptor = factor(main_descriptor),
    secondary_descriptor=factor(secondary_descriptor),
    additional_descriptor=factor(additional_descriptor),
    labmethod = factor(labmethod),
    crop=factor(crop),
    # scale continuous predictors for easier interpretation
    temp_s = scale(bio1_temp),
    precip_s = scale(bio12_precip)
  )
soc_main$year<-as.numeric(soc_main$year)
```

## Graph the country
```{r}
soc_main %>%
  group_by(country, year) %>%
  summarise(mean_SOC = mean(log_SOC_gKg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!country %in% c("East Timor", "Myanmar")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean_SOC)) +
  geom_line() + geom_smooth(linetype=0)+
  facet_wrap(~country) +
  labs(
    title = "SOC Concentration Over Time by Country",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Average ln(SOC) (g/kg)"
  ) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    # legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = seq(0, 7, 2))
```


# RANDOM FOREST
```{r}
library(randomForest)
```

## RF depth 0-30
```{r}
#sum(complete.cases(depth_030))
rf_model_d1 <- randomForest(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ temp_s + precip_s + secondary_descriptor,
  data = depth_030, 
  na.action = na.omit,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 500)

# View importance
randomForest::importance(rf_model_d1)
randomForest::varImpPlot(rf_model_d1)
```

# RF depth 30-60
```{r}
# Random forest model
rf_model_d2 <- randomForest(
  log_SOC_gKg ~  temp_s + precip_s +secondary_descriptor,
  data = depth_3060,
  na.action = na.omit,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 500)

# View importance
randomForest::importance(rf_model_d2)
randomForest::varImpPlot(rf_model_d2)
```

## RF depth 60
```{r}
# Random forest model
rf_model_d3 <- randomForest(
  log_SOC_gKg ~  temp_s + precip_s + secondary_descriptor,
  data = depth_60,
  na.action = na.omit,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 500)

# View importance
randomForest::importance(rf_model_d3)
randomForest::varImpPlot(rf_model_d3)
```

## dont run this
```{r}
soc_main$secondary_descriptor <- as.factor(soc_main$secondary_descriptor)
gkg <- subset(soc_main, !is.na(log_SOC_gKg))
gkg <- gkg %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(depth_class),
                !is.na(secondary_descriptor),
                !is.na(temp_s),
                !is.na(precip_s),
                !is.na(log_SOC_gKg))

# Random forest model
rf_model <- randomForest(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ depth_class + secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s,
  data = gkg,
  na.action = na.omit,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 500
)

# View importance
randomForest::importance(rf_model)
randomForest::varImpPlot(rf_model)
```

# Random Forest evaluation
```{r}
# R² for depth 1
rf_r2_d1 <- 1 - (rf_model_d1$mse[rf_model_d1$ntree] / var(depth_030$log_SOC_gKg, na.rm = TRUE))
print(paste("Random Forest R²:", round(rf_r2_d1, 3)))
#randomForest::importance(rf_model_d1)
# R2 for depth 2
rf_r2_d2 <- 1 - (rf_model_d2$mse[rf_model_d2$ntree] / var(depth_3060$log_SOC_gKg, na.rm = TRUE))
print(paste("Random Forest R²:", round(rf_r2_d2, 3)))
# R3 for depth 3
rf_r2_d3 <- 1 - (rf_model_d3$mse[rf_model_d3$ntree] / var(depth_60$log_SOC_gKg, na.rm = TRUE))
print(paste("Random Forest R²:", round(rf_r2_d3, 3)))
```

79% the variation in Soil Organic Carbon (SOC) is explained by this random forest model

```{r}
# RMSE
rf_rmse_d1 <- sqrt(rf_model_d1$mse[rf_model_d1$ntree])
rf_rmse_d2 <- sqrt(rf_model_d2$mse[rf_model_d1$ntree])
rf_rmse_d3 <- sqrt(rf_model_d3$mse[rf_model_d1$ntree])

print(paste("Random Forest RMSE:", round(rf_rmse_d1, 3)))
print(paste("Random Forest RMSE:", round(rf_rmse_d2, 3)))
print(paste("Random Forest RMSE:", round(rf_rmse_d3, 3)))
```

# Don't run this
```{r}
# Predicted vs observed
pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = gkg)
obs <- gkg$log_SOC_gKg

plot(obs, pred,
     xlab = "Observed log(SOC g/kg)",
     ylab = "Predicted log(SOC g/kg)",
     main = "Random Forest Model Fit for Soil Organic Carbon",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0.2, 0.4, 0.7, 0.4))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}
# Predicted vs observed
pred_d1 <- predict(rf_model_d1, newdata = depth_030)
obs_d1 <- depth_030$log_SOC_gKg

plot(obs_d1, pred_d1,
     xlab = "Observed log(SOC g/kg)",
     ylab = "Predicted log(SOC g/kg)",
     main = "Random Forest Model Fit for Soil Organic Carbon, depth 0-30cm, 
     R2 = 0.88",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0.2, 0.4, 0.7, 0.4)) + abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}
importance_df_d1 <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(randomForest::importance(rf_model_d1)),
  Importance = randomForest::importance(rf_model_d1)[, 1])
summary(importance_df_d1)
importance_df_d1 <- importance_df_d1 %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      temp_s = "Temperature",
      precip_s = "Precipitation",
      secondary_descriptor = "Management"))

ggplot(importance_df_d1, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1b9e77", width = 0.4) +
  #scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) +
  labs(
    x = "Variable",
    y = "% Increase in MSE",
    title = "Variable Importance in SOC Prediction,\nDepth 0–30 cm (Random Forest)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),aspect.ratio = 1/1)

```

```{r}
# Predicted vs observed
pred_d2 <- predict(rf_model_d2, newdata = depth_3060)
obs_d2 <- depth_3060$log_SOC_gKg

plot(obs_d2, pred_d2,
     xlab = "Observed log(SOC g/kg)",
     ylab = "Predicted log(SOC g/kg)",
     main = "Random Forest Model Fit for Soil Organic Carbon, depth 30-60cm, 
     R2=0.74",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0.2, 0.4, 0.7, 0.4)) + abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}
importance_df_d2 <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(randomForest::importance(rf_model_d2)),
  Importance = randomForest::importance(rf_model_d2)[, 1])

importance_df_d2 <- importance_df_d2 %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      temp_s = "Temperature",
      precip_s = "Precipitation",
      secondary_descriptor = "Management"))

ggplot(importance_df_d2, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#d95f02", width = 0.4) +
  labs(
    x = "Variable",
    y = "% Increase in MSE",
    title = "Variable Importance in SOC Prediction,\nDepth 30–60 cm (Random Forest)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),aspect.ratio = 1/1)
```

```{r}
# Predicted vs observed
pred_d3 <- predict(rf_model_d3, newdata = depth_60)
obs_d3 <- depth_60$log_SOC_gKg

plot(obs_d3, pred_d3,
     xlab = "Observed log(SOC g/kg)",
     ylab = "Predicted log(SOC g/kg)",
     main = "Random Forest Model Fit for Soil Organic Carbon, depth 60+cm,
     R2=0.78",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0.2, 0.4, 0.7, 0.4)) + abline(0, 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}
importance_df_d3 <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(randomForest::importance(rf_model_d3)),
  Importance = randomForest::importance(rf_model_d3)[, 1])

importance_df_d3 <- importance_df_d3 %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      temp_s = "Temperature",
      precip_s = "Precipitation",
      secondary_descriptor = "Management factors"))

ggplot(importance_df_d3, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#7570b3", width = 0.4) +
  labs(
    x = "Variable",
    y = "% Increase in MSE",
    title = "Variable Importance in SOC Prediction,\nDepth 60+ cm (Random Forest)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),aspect.ratio = 1/1)
```




# 1. Testing mixed-effect model
- Soil depth has the strongest negative effect on SOC concentration: SOC decreases significantly with depth 30–60 cm: –0.94 log units, 60+ cm: –1.44 log units (p < 0.001). → Indicates topsoil holds most carbon storage.

- Land management descriptors varied in impact: Tillage (–0.54, p < 0.001) and Other managements (–1.18, p < 0.001) significantly reduced SOC. Annual cropping systems also slightly decreased SOC (–0.20, p = 0.04). Perennial systems retained more SOC than annual ones (p = 0.002). Fertilization and organic management alone showed no significant effect, implying management context matters more than single interventions.

- Climatic drivers: Precipitation was strongly positive (+0.56, p < 0.001) → wetter regions promote carbon accumulation. Temperature had no significant linear effect (p = 0.24), possibly masked by co-variation with precipitation or elevation.

-Random country effects (SD = 0.43) indicate moderate variability between national-level contexts (e.g., soil types, measurement methods).

# 1A - Filtering Land Use
```{r}
soc_landuse <-soc_main %>% filter(main_descriptor=="Land use", !is.na(log_SOC_gKg)) %>% select(
  id, year, lon, lat, location, country, main_descriptor, secondary_descriptor, temp_s, precip_s, depth_class, log_SOC_gKg)
#explore
ggplot(na.omit(soc_landuse)) + aes(x=factor(secondary_descriptor), y=log_SOC_gKg, fill=factor(secondary_descriptor)) + facet_wrap(~depth_class)+
  geom_boxplot()+labs( title="Distribution of SOC by land use and depth", x="Land use",y="Log SOC")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Agroforestry" = "brown",
    "Annual" = "darkgreen",
    "Perennial"="beige" )) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
landuse <-lm(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + depth_class, data = soc_landuse)
summary(landuse)
```

```{r}
summary(model3_60)
confint(model3_60, method = "profile")
```


# 1.1 By Depth
```{r}
#Depth 0-30
model1_030<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_030, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #management
model2_030<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_030, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #tillage_binary
model3_030<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_030, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #fertilization_binary
# Depth 30-60
model1_3060<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_3060, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #management
model2_3060<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_3060, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #tillage_binary
model3_3060<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_3060, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #fertilization_binary
#Depth 30-60
model1_60<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_60, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #management
#model2_60<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_60, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #tillage_binary
model3_60<-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country) + (1|year), data = depth_60, REML = FALSE, na.action = na.omit) #fertilization_binary

model_list <- list(
  model1_030 = model1_030,
  model2_030 = model2_030,
  model3_030 = model3_030,
  model1_3060 = model1_3060,
  model2_3060 = model2_3060,
  model3_3060 = model3_3060,
  model1_60 = model1_60,
  model3_60 = model3_60)

extract_coefs <- function(model, model_name){
  est <- fixef(model)
  ci <- confint(model, parm = names(est), method = "Wald")
  df <- data.frame(
    term = names(est),
    estimate = est,
    conf.low = ci[,1],
    conf.high = ci[,2],
    Model = model_name
  )
  return(df)}
coef_table <- do.call(rbind,
  lapply(names(model_list), function(name){
    extract_coefs(model_list[[name]], name)
  })
)

library(dplyr)

coef_table <- coef_table %>%
  mutate(
    Depth = case_when(
      grepl("030", Model) ~ "0-30",
      grepl("3060", Model) ~ "30-60",
      grepl("_60", Model) ~ "60+"
    ),
    Practice = case_when(
      grepl("model1", Model) ~ "Land use",
      grepl("model2", Model) ~ "Tillage",
      grepl("model3", Model) ~ "Fertilization"
    )
  )
coef_plot <- coef_table %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")
coef_plot <- coef_plot %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = case_when(
      term == "temp_s" ~ "Temperature",
      term == "precip_s" ~ "Precipitation",
      term == "tillage_intensity" ~ "Tillage",
      term == "fert_intensity" ~ "Fertilization",
      grepl("secondary_descriptor", term) ~ "Land use",
      TRUE ~ term
    )
  )
coef_plot <- coef_plot %>%

  mutate(
    Significant = ifelse(conf.low * conf.high > 0, "Yes", "No")
  )
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(coef_plot, aes(y = Variable, x = estimate, color = Depth)) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height = 0.25
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Significant)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Yes" = 4, "No" = 2)) +
  labs(
    x = "Slope estimate",
    y = "",
    title = "Drivers of Soil Organic Carbon across Soil Depths",
    subtitle = "Mixed-effects model estimates with 95% confidence intervals"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
```{r}
coef_plot$Variable <- gsub("secondary_descriptor", "Land use:", coef_plot$Variable)
coef_plot <- coef_plot %>%
  mutate(
    Label = paste(Depth, Variable))
coef_plot$Depth <- factor(
  coef_plot$Depth,
  levels = c("60+", "30-60", "0-30"))
coef_plot <- coef_plot %>%
  arrange(Depth, Variable)
coef_plot$Variable <- gsub("secondary_descriptor", "Land use:", coef_plot$Variable)
coef_plot$Label <- factor(coef_plot$Label, levels = unique(coef_plot$Label))
ggplot(coef_plot, aes(y = Label, x = estimate, color = Depth)) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height = 0.25,
    size = 0.8
  ) +

  geom_point(aes(size = Significant)) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey40"
  ) +

  scale_size_manual(values = c("Yes" = 4, "No" = 2)) +

  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "60+" = "#1b9e77",
    "30-60" = "#d95f02",
    "0-30" = "#7570b3"
  )) +

  labs(
    x = "Slope Estimate (Effect Size)",
    y = "",
    color = "Depth (cm)",
    size = "Significant?",
    title = "Effect of Management & Climate on SOC\nby Depth Class",
    subtitle = "Points = Estimate\nHorizontal lines = 95% CI"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +

  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```



```{r}
# Split by depth
depth_030<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="0-30")
depth_3060<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="30-60")
depth_60<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="60+")

# depth 0-30 = 3107
# depth 30-60 = 565
# depth 60+ = 647
table(soc_main$depth_class)
```

# 1.1.2 Depth 
```{r}
depth1 <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  secondary_descriptor  + temp_s + precip_s + (1 |country)+(1|year),
  data = depth_030,
  na.action = na.omit,
  REML = FALSE
)
summary(depth1)
#library(MuMIn)
#r.squaredGLMM(depth1)
#confint(depth1, method = "Wald")
#summary(predict(depth1, re.form = NULL, se.fit = TRUE))
```
The following linear mixed-effects model was fitted:

$$
\log(\text{SOC}_{i}) =
\beta_0
+ \beta_1 \text{SecondaryDescriptor}_{i}
+ \beta_2 \text{Temp}_{i}
+ \beta_3 \text{Precip}_{i}
+ u_{\text{country}[i]}
+ v_{\text{year}[i]}
+ \varepsilon_i
$$
where $u_{\text{country}} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2_{\text{country}})$,
$v_{\text{year}} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2_{\text{year}})$,
and $\varepsilon_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)$



```{r}
depth2 <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  secondary_descriptor  + temp_s + precip_s + (1 |country)+(1|year),
  data = depth_3060,
  na.action = na.omit,
  REML = FALSE
)
summary(depth2)
r.squaredGLMM(depth2)
confint(depth2, method = "Wald")
confint(depth2, method = "profile")
```

```{r}
depth3 <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  secondary_descriptor  + temp_s + precip_s + (1 |country)+(1|year),
  data = depth_60,
  na.action = na.omit,
  REML = FALSE
)
summary(depth3)
#r.squaredGLMM(depth3)
#confint(depth3, method = "Wald")
confint(depth3, method = "profile")
```

# Create a dataset that combines depth-level linear mixed model values for better visuals
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
lmm_data <- tribble(
  ~Name, ~Slope, ~LCI, ~UCI, ~Significant,
  "0-30 Annual", -0.44, -0.67, -0.21, "Yes",
  "30-60 Annual", -1.29, -1.85, -0.74, "Yes",
  "60+ Annual", -0.22, -0.53,  0.08, "No",
  "0-30 Perennial", -0.48, -0.69, -0.27, "Yes",
  "30-60 Perennial", -0.83, -1.4, -0.26, "Yes",
  "60+ Perennial", -0.23, -0.52, 0.07, "No",
  "0-30 Tillage", -0.02, -0.16, 0.11, "No",
  "30-60 Tillage", -0.15, -0.42, 0.12, "Yes",
  "0-30 Fertilization", 0.19, 0.09, 0.29, "Yes",
  "30-60 Fertilization", 0.3, -0.14, 0.61, "No",
  "60+ Fertilization",  0.4, -0.003, 0.8, "No")

#Depth seperate
lmm_data <- lmm_data %>%
  separate(Name, into = c("Depth", "Practice"), sep = " ", remove = FALSE)

lmm_data$Depth <- factor(lmm_data$Depth, levels = c("60+", "30-60", "0-30"))
lmm_data <- lmm_data %>%
  arrange(Depth, Practice)

lmm_data$Significant <- factor(lmm_data$Significant, levels = c("Yes", "No"))
```

# old one
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
lmm_data <- tribble(
  ~Name, ~Predicted_val, ~Slope, ~LCI, ~UCI, ~Significant,
  "0-30 Annual", 3.23672, -0.44, -0.67, -0.21, "Yes",
  "30-60 Annual", 2.43251, -1.29, -1.85, -0.74, "Yes",
  "60+ Annual", 1.39467, -0.22, -0.53,  0.08, "No",
  "0-30 Perennial", 3.20268, -0.48, -0.69, -0.27, "Yes",
  "30-60 Perennial", 2.898051, -0.83, -1.4, -0.26, "Yes",
  "60+ Perennial", 1.39223, -0.23, -0.52, 0.06, "No",
  "0-30 Tillage", 3.18954, -0.49, -0.78, -0.20, "Yes",
  "30-60 Tillage", 2.05003, -1.68, -2.25, -1.10, "Yes",
  "60+ Tillage", 2.36755, 0.68, 0.17, 1.18, "Yes",
  "0-30 Fertilization", 3.30969, -0.37, -0.59, -0.15, "Yes",
  "30-60 Fertilization", 1.920411, -1.80, -2.36, -1.25, "Yes",
  "60+ Fertilization", 1.79499, 0.17, -0.18, 0.53, "No",
  "0-30 Precipitation", 4.17681, 0.49, 0.42, 0.57, "Yes",
  "30-60 Precipitation", 3.529517, -0.198, -0.44, 0.05, "No",
  "60+ Precipitation", 1.27835, -0.34, -0.49, -0.20, "Yes",
  "0-30 Temperature", 3.78635, 0.104, 0.05, 0.15, "Yes",
  "30-60 Temperature", 3.724062, -0.004, -0.16, 0.15, "No",
  "60+ Temperature", 1.12889, -0.49, -0.61, -0.37, "Yes"
)

#Depth seperate
lmm_data <- lmm_data %>%
  separate(Name, into = c("Depth", "Practice"), sep = " ", remove = FALSE)

lmm_data$Depth <- factor(lmm_data$Depth, levels = c("60+", "30-60", "0-30"))
lmm_data <- lmm_data %>%
  arrange(Depth, Practice)

lmm_data$Significant <- factor(lmm_data$Significant, levels = c("Yes", "No"))
```

# Visualizing
```{r}
# Visualizing
ggplot(lmm_data, aes(y = Name, x = Slope, color = Depth)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LCI, xmax = UCI), height = 0.25, size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Significant)) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Yes" = 4, "No" = 2)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "60+" = "#1b9e77",
    "30-60" = "#d95f02",
    "0-30"  = "#7570b3"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "Slope Estimate (Effect Size)",
    y = "",
    color = "Depth (cm)",
    size = "Significant?",
    title = "Effect of Management & Climate on SOC\nby Depth Class",
    subtitle = "Points = Estimate\nHorizontal lines = 95% CI"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```


## 2. Set up SEM
```{r}
#fert_d0<- depth_3060 %>% filter (secondary_descriptor =="Fertilization")
#table(depth_60$secondary_descriptor)
#unique_030 <- depth_60 %>% distinct()
#depth_030 %>%
#  summarise(count = sum(grepl("Others", additional_descriptor)))

#depth_60 %>%
 # filter(secondary_descriptor == "Perennial") %>%
  #count(additional_descriptor)
```


# Create dummy variable for secondary_descriptors --> this needs to be done differently beacause of so many different categories in this. 

Management intensity is not universally defined across all management systems. Therefore, intensity variables were constructed only for management types where intensity variation is meaningful (e.g., fertilization and tillage), following standard practice in soil management literature.

## 2.1. Fertilization intensity
```{r}
soc_main <- soc_main %>% mutate(
  fert_intensity = case_when(
  secondary_descriptor=="Fertilization" &
    str_detect(additional_descriptor,regex("without",ignore_case=TRUE))~0,
  secondary_descriptor=="Fertilization"&
    str_detect(additional_descriptor,regex("with",ignore_case=TRUE))~1,
  secondary_descriptor=="Fertilization" ~1,
  TRUE~ NA_real_))
```


## 2.2. Tillage intensity
```{r}
soc_main <- soc_main %>%
  mutate(
    tillage_intensity = case_when(
      secondary_descriptor == "Tillage" &
        str_detect(additional_descriptor, regex("No tillage", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 0,
      secondary_descriptor == "Tillage" &
        str_detect(additional_descriptor, regex("Minimum tillage", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 1,
      secondary_descriptor == "Tillage" &
        str_detect(additional_descriptor, regex("Reduced tillage", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 1,
      secondary_descriptor == "Tillage" &
        str_detect(additional_descriptor, regex("Conventional tillage", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ 1,
      secondary_descriptor == "Tillage" ~ 1, 
      TRUE ~ NA_real_))
```


# 3. Structural Equation Modeling (SEM)
```{r}
#depth dataset again
depth_030<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="0-30")
depth_3060<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="30-60")
depth_60<-subset(soc_main, soc_main$depth_class=="60+")
#we have to bootstrap by hand manually
#df = 0 is not a problem is not the same at there are no indirect effect, is not the model error. We cannot evaluate the Global Goodness of Fit. 
#should do boostrap to get indirect relationship between Y and M
#dash line is not significant
```

## Ferilizer intensity by depth
### Depth 0-30, fertilizer intensity
```{r}
m_fert_030 <- lmer(fert_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_030)
m_soc_030_fert <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s +
                    (1 | country), data = depth_030)
sem_030_fert <- psem(m_fert_030,m_soc_030_fert)
summary(sem_030_fert)
plot(sem_030_fert)
```

### Depth 30-60, fertilization intensity
```{r}
m_fert_3060 <- lmer(fert_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_3060)
m_soc_3060_fert <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s +
                    (1 | country), data = depth_3060)

sem_3060_fert <- psem(m_fert_3060,m_soc_3060_fert)
summary(sem_3060_fert)
plot(sem_3060_fert)
```


### Depth 60+, fertilizer intensity
```{r}
m_fert_60 <- lmer(fert_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_60)
m_soc_60_fert <- lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~  fert_intensity + temp_s + precip_s +
                    (1 | country), data = depth_60)

sem_60_fert <- psem(m_fert_60,m_soc_60_fert)
summary(sem_60_fert)
plot(sem_60_fert)
```

### Land Use at depth 30

```{r}

library(dplyr)
landuse <- soc_main %>%
  filter(main_descriptor == "Land use",
         depth_class == "0-30") %>%
  mutate(
    country = as.factor(country),
    land_use = case_when(
      secondary_descriptor == "Annual" ~ 1,
      secondary_descriptor == "Perennial" ~ 2,
      secondary_descriptor == "Agroforestry" ~ 3
    )
  ) %>%
  select(id, country, log_SOC_gKg,
         land_use, temp_s, precip_s) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

m_landuse <- lmer(
  land_use ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country),
  data = landuse
)
m_SOC <- lmer(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ land_use + temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country),
  data = landuse
)

sem_landuse <- psem(
  m_landuse,
  m_SOC
)
summary(sem_landuse)
plot(sem_landuse)
```

# Depth 0-30, emmeans for Land Use
```{r}
depth_030_landuse<-subset(soc_landuse, soc_landuse$depth_class=="0-30")
depth_030_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- factor(depth_030_landuse$secondary_descriptor)
depth_030_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- relevel(depth_030_landuse$secondary_descriptor, ref = "Agroforestry")

m_soc_030_landuse <- lmer(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country),
  data = depth_030_landuse)

emm1 <- emmeans(m_soc_030_landuse, ~ secondary_descriptor)
summary(emm1)

emm_df1 <- as.data.frame(emm1)
ggplot(emm_df1,
       aes(x = secondary_descriptor,
           y = emmean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL,
                    ymax = upper.CL),
                width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Land Use",
    y = "Predicted log(SOC)",
    title = "0–30 cm: SOC by Land Use")
```
emmeans plot display adjusted means, model-predicted SOG for each land-use type, holding temperature and precipitation constant. Dot = predicted, 95% CI. 
Landuse: Annual = highest, agroforestry = smallest, but this is not statistically significnace (p=0.8)
Climate (as known): temp: -0.06, precpip: 0.22 (significant)

# Depth 30-60, emmeans for Land Use
```{r}
depth_3060_landuse<-subset(soc_landuse, soc_landuse$depth_class=="30-60")
depth_3060_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- factor(depth_3060_landuse$secondary_descriptor)
depth_3060_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- relevel(depth_3060_landuse$secondary_descriptor, ref = "Agroforestry")

m_soc_3060_landuse <- lmer(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country),
  data = depth_3060_landuse)

emm2 <- emmeans(m_soc_3060_landuse, ~ secondary_descriptor)
summary(emm2)

emm_df2 <- as.data.frame(emm2)
ggplot(emm_df2,
       aes(x = secondary_descriptor,
           y = emmean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL,
                    ymax = upper.CL),
                width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Land Use",
    y = "Predicted log(SOC)",
    title = "30–60 cm: SOC by Land Use")
```

# Depth 60+, emmeans for Land Use
```{r}
depth_60_landuse<-subset(soc_landuse, soc_landuse$depth_class=="60+")
depth_60_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- factor(depth_60_landuse$secondary_descriptor)
depth_60_landuse$secondary_descriptor <- relevel(depth_60_landuse$secondary_descriptor, ref = "Agroforestry")

m_soc_60_landuse <- lmer(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s + (1|country),
  data = depth_60_landuse)

emm3 <- emmeans(m_soc_60_landuse, ~ secondary_descriptor)
summary(emm3)

emm_df3 <- as.data.frame(emm3)
ggplot(emm_df3,
       aes(x = secondary_descriptor,
           y = emmean)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL,
                    ymax = upper.CL),
                width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Land Use",
    y = "Predicted log(SOC)",
    title = "60+ cm: SOC by Land Use")
```

## Depth 0-30, tillage intensity
```{r}
m_till_030 <- lmer(tillage_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_030)
m_soc_030_till <-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_030)

sem_030_till <- psem(m_till_030,m_soc_030_till)

summary(sem_030_till)
plot(sem_030_till)
```


```{r}
# Customized for publication (requires 'DiagrammeR')
library(DiagrammeR)
install.packages("ggsem")
library(ggsem)
plot(sem_030_till, 
     node_attrs = list(shape = "box", color = "black"),
     edge_attrs = list(color = "grey"))

coefs_df <- coefs(sem_030_till, standardize = "scale")

ggplot(coefs_df,
       aes(x = Predictor,
           y = Std.Estimate,
           fill = Response)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(y = "Standardized Effect",
       title = "SEM Standardized Path Coefficients (0–30 cm)")
```
```{r}
library(lavaan)

model <- '
  tillage_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s
  log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s
'

fit <- sem(model, data = depth_030)

library(semPlot)
semPaths(fit,
         what = "std",
         layout = "tree",
         edge.label.cex = 1.2,
         sizeMan = 8)
```

## Depth 30-60, tillage intensity
```{r}
m_till_3060 <- lmer(tillage_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_3060)
m_soc_3060_till <-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity + temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_3060)

sem_3060_till <- psem(m_till_3060,m_soc_3060_till)

summary(sem_3060_till)
plot(sem_3060_till)
```


## Depth 60+, tillage intensity ## DROP
```{r}
m_till_60 <- lmer(tillage_intensity ~ temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_60)
m_soc_60_till <-lmer(log_SOC_gKg ~ tillage_intensity * temp_s + precip_s + (1 | country), data = depth_60)

sem_60_till <- psem(m_till_60,m_soc_60_till)

summary(sem_60_till)
plot(sem_60_till)
```


significant indirect relationship between M and Y (land management practices and SOC)

```{r}
m1.1 <- lm(secondary_num ~ temp_s + precip_s, data = depth_data[[2]])
# M~X
m2.2 <- lm(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_num + temp_s +precip_s, data = depth_data[[2]])
# Y~M+X

sem_model_2 <- psem(m1.1, m2.2)

# Summary
summary(sem_model_2)

plot(sem_model_2)
```

```{r}
m1.3 <- lm(secondary_num ~ temp_s + precip_s, data = depth_data[[3]])
# M~X
m2.3 <- lm(log_SOC_gKg ~ secondary_num + temp_s +precip_s, data = depth_data[[3]])
# Y~M+X

sem_model_3 <- psem(m1.3, m2.3)

# Summary
summary(sem_model_3)

plot(sem_model_3)
```


# Example of a decision tree visualization
```{r}
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)

# Example
rf_model <- randomForest(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ depth_class + secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s,
  data = depth_data[[1]],
  na.action = na.omit,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 500
)

fit_rpart <- rpart(
  log_SOC_gKg ~ depth_class + secondary_descriptor + temp_s + precip_s,
  data = depth_data[[1]],
  method = "anova"
)

# Visualize decision structure
rpart.plot(fit_rpart, type = 2, extra = 101, fallen.leaves = TRUE,
           main = "Decision Tree for Predicting SOC (g/kg)")

```

## EDA Code
# Distribution of published years of articles
```{r}
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$year))
```


# Distribution of country's article
```{r}
ggplot(soc_data_climate, aes(x = country)) +
  geom_bar()+theme_minimal()
```

# Distribution of crops
won't use crop as a variable in research
```{r}
crop_counts <- soc_data_climate %>%
  count(crop, sort = TRUE)

ggplot(crop_counts %>% slice_max(n, n = 20), aes(x = reorder(crop, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Crop / System", y = "Count", title = "Top 20 Reported Crops/Systems") +
  theme_minimal()
```
